metadata.name: Terraform-Aligned Production Deployment
metadata.purpose: Enterprise Terraform-managed blue-green deployment with GitHub Workflow orchestration and DigitalOcean load balancer integration
metadata.author: Ectropy Infrastructure Team

executiveSummary.description: Complete Terraform-managed production deployment workflow integrating Infrastructure as Code, GitHub Actions workflows, and DigitalOcean load balancer orchestration for zero-downtime blue-green deployments
keyChanges: Terraform provisions and validates production infrastructure
GitHub Variables populated from Terraform outputs
DigitalOcean load balancer updated via Terraform for traffic cutover
Complete state validation at each phase
Automated rollback capability at infrastructure and application layers
prerequisites: Terraform v1.0+ installed with DigitalOcean provider configured
GitHub CLI (gh) authenticated with repository access
Production droplets deployed via Terraform (blue and green)

impact.downtime: Zero - load balancer cutover is seamless

secrets.PROD_SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: SSH key for production droplet access

phases.name: Pre-Deployment Infrastructure Validation
phases.description: Validate Terraform state and infrastructure readiness

steps.name: Verify Terraform state backend connectivity
steps.command: cd infrastructure/terraform && terraform init
steps.purpose: Ensure Terraform can access remote state in DigitalOcean Spaces

onFailure.message: Cannot access Terraform state backend

steps.name: Validate Terraform plan has no drift
steps.command: terraform plan -detailed-exitcode
steps.purpose: Ensure infrastructure matches declared state (no unexpected changes)

validation.criteria: Exit code 0 = no changes, 2 = changes detected (fail)

onFailure.message: Infrastructure drift detected - review before deployment

steps.name: Verify production droplets are healthy
commands: doctl compute droplet get production-blue --format ID,Status,PublicIPv4
doctl compute droplet get production-green --format ID,Status,PublicIPv4
steps.purpose: Confirm both blue and green droplets are active

onFailure.message: Production droplets are not healthy

steps.name: Check load balancer health
steps.command: doctl compute load-balancer get ectropy-production-lb-v2 --format ID,Status,IP
steps.purpose: Verify load balancer is operational

validation.criteria: Load balancer Status must be active

onFailure.message: Load balancer is not healthy

steps.name: Validate GitHub Variables match Terraform outputs
steps.purpose: Ensure GitHub workflow has correct IP addresses

validation.criteria: GitHub Variables must match Terraform outputs

onFailure.message: GitHub Variables out of sync - executing sync step

steps.name: Sync GitHub Variables from Terraform (if needed)
commands: gh variable set PROD_BLUE_IP --body "$(terraform output -raw prod_blue_ip)"
gh variable set PROD_GREEN_IP --body "$(terraform output -raw prod_green_ip)"
steps.purpose: Update GitHub Variables to match current Terraform state

onFailure.message: Failed to sync GitHub Variables - manual intervention required

phases.name: Determine Active and Standby Colors
phases.description: Query load balancer to identify current active droplet and standby target

steps.name: Query load balancer backend pool
steps.command: doctl compute load-balancer get ectropy-production-lb-v2 --format ForwardingRules,HealthCheck | jq .forwarding_rules[].droplet_ids[]
steps.purpose: Identify which droplet is currently receiving production traffic

validation.criteria: Returns droplet ID currently in backend pool

onFailure.message: Cannot determine active droplet

steps.name: Map droplet ID to color (blue or green)
steps.command: doctl compute droplet list --format ID,Name | grep production
steps.purpose: Determine if blue or green is active

validation.criteria: Active color identified, standby color is the opposite

onFailure.message: Cannot map droplet to blue/green color

steps.name: Set deployment variables
steps.purpose: Configure deployment target (standby droplet)

onFailure.message: Failed to set deployment variables

phases.name: GitHub Workflow Execution - Application Deployment
phases.description: Trigger and monitor deploy-production.yml workflow to deploy application to standby droplet

steps.name: Trigger deploy-production.yml workflow
steps.command: gh workflow run deploy-production.yml --ref main --field target_color=$STANDBY_COLOR
steps.purpose: Start GitHub Actions workflow to deploy to standby droplet

onFailure.message: Failed to trigger deployment workflow

steps.name: Monitor workflow execution
steps.command: gh run watch $(gh run list --workflow=deploy-production.yml --limit 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId')
steps.purpose: Track deployment progress and wait for completion

validation.criteria: Workflow must complete with success status

onFailure.message: Deployment workflow failed - check workflow logs

steps.name: Verify standby droplet health after deployment
steps.command: curl -sf http://${STANDBY_DROPLET_IP}/lb-health
steps.purpose: Confirm standby droplet is serving traffic and healthy

onFailure.message: Standby droplet failed health check after deployment

steps.name: Execute smoke tests on standby droplet
steps.purpose: Validate application endpoints before traffic cutover

onFailure.message: Smoke tests failed on standby droplet

phases.name: Terraform-Managed Load Balancer Cutover
phases.description: Update load balancer backend pool via Terraform to switch traffic to standby droplet

steps.name: Update Terraform load balancer configuration
commands: # Update load balancer backend to point to standby droplet
steps.purpose: Modify load balancer backend pool to route traffic to newly deployed droplet

validation.criteria: Terraform apply completes successfully with 1 resource changed

onFailure.message: Terraform apply failed - load balancer not updated

notes: This step updates the load balancer's backend pool
Traffic is seamlessly transitioned from active to standby droplet
Health checks ensure only healthy backends receive traffic

steps.name: Wait for load balancer to recognize new backend
steps.command: sleep 30
steps.purpose: Allow load balancer health checks to validate new backend

onFailure.message: Wait interrupted but proceeding

steps.name: Verify load balancer health check status
steps.command: doctl compute load-balancer get ectropy-production-lb-v2 --format HealthCheck | jq .health_check
steps.purpose: Confirm load balancer is successfully routing to new backend

validation.criteria: Health check shows healthy status for new backend

onFailure.message: Load balancer health check failed for new backend

phases.name: Post-Cutover Production Validation
phases.description: Verify production is serving traffic from new deployment

steps.name: Test production URL via load balancer
steps.command: curl -sf https://ectropy.ai/lb-health
steps.purpose: Verify production load balancer is responding

onFailure.message: Production health check FAILED - initiating rollback

steps.name: Execute production smoke tests
steps.purpose: Validate all production endpoints after cutover

onFailure.message: Production smoke tests failed - initiating rollback

steps.name: Monitor error rates for 5 minutes
steps.command: # Monitor logs and health endpoints for anomalies
steps.purpose: Observe production behavior under real traffic

validation.criteria: No significant error rate increase, latency within normal range

onFailure.message: Production showing elevated errors - initiating rollback

phases.name: Rollback Procedure (If Needed)
phases.description: Automated rollback to revert load balancer to previous backend

steps.name: Revert Terraform load balancer configuration
steps.purpose: Revert load balancer backend to previous active droplet

onFailure.message: CRITICAL: Automated rollback failed - manual intervention required

steps.name: Verify rollback health
steps.command: curl -sf https://ectropy.ai/lb-health
steps.purpose: Confirm production is back to stable state

onFailure.message: CRITICAL: Rollback verification failed - escalate to on-call

steps.name: Document rollback reason
steps.command: # Create incident log with failure details
steps.purpose: Capture root cause for post-mortem analysis

phases.name: Post-Deployment Terraform State Validation
phases.description: Confirm Terraform state reflects successful deployment

steps.name: Run Terraform plan to confirm no drift
steps.command: cd infrastructure/terraform && terraform plan -detailed-exitcode
steps.purpose: Verify infrastructure state is consistent after deployment

onFailure.message: Infrastructure drift detected post-deployment - review recommended

steps.name: Update infrastructure catalog
steps.command: # Update docs/INFRASTRUCTURE_CATALOG.md with deployment details
steps.purpose: Document current production state

steps.name: Tag Terraform state with deployment version
steps.command: # Optional: Tag Terraform state for rollback reference
steps.purpose: Create recovery point for future rollbacks

triggerConditions: Load balancer health check failure post-cutover (Phase 4)
rollback.decisionMaker: Automated with manual escalation if rollback fails

steps.name: Revert Terraform load balancer backend
steps.command: terraform apply -target=digitalocean_loadbalancer.production -auto-approve
steps.purpose: Switch load balancer back to previous active droplet

steps.name: Verify production health
steps.command: curl -sf https://ectropy.ai/lb-health
steps.purpose: Confirm rollback successful

steps.name: Create incident log
steps.command: # Document rollback reason and failure analysis
steps.purpose: Capture details for post-mortem

successCriteria.criterion: GitHub Variables synced with Terraform outputs
No elevated error rates in first 5 minutes

auditTrail.terraformStateLogs: Preserved in DigitalOcean Spaces with versioning
auditTrail.githubWorkflowLogs: Retained in GitHub Actions for 90 days
auditTrail.infrastructureLogs: Load balancer and droplet logs in DigitalOcean

soc2Compliance.changeControl: All changes via Terraform and GitHub workflows
soc2Compliance.accessControl: GitHub secrets for credentials, no plaintext secrets
soc2Compliance.auditability: Complete audit trail from Terraform to production

knownIssues.title: Load balancer update may cause brief connection disruption
knownIssues.impact: Existing connections may experience < 1s disruption during backend pool update
knownIssues.mitigation: Load balancer health checks ensure only healthy backends receive traffic

knownIssues.title: Terraform state lock contention if multiple operators
knownIssues.impact: Concurrent Terraform operations will fail with state lock error
knownIssues.mitigation: Coordinate deployments, use Terraform workspace locking
knownIssues.action: Ensure only one deployment runs at a time

terraform.name: DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN
terraform.description: DigitalOcean API token for Terraform provider

terraform.name: TF_STATE_BUCKET
terraform.description: DigitalOcean Spaces bucket for Terraform state

supportedQueries.query: How does Terraform coordinate with GitHub workflows?
supportedQueries.returns: Terraform outputs populate GitHub Variables, workflows use variables for deployment

supportedQueries.query: What is the production deployment process?
supportedQueries.returns: 7-phase process: Terraform validation → GitHub workflow deployment → Load balancer cutover → Production validation
Automated Terraform revert of load balancer backend pool to previous droplet
